payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Task claiming function enabling children to accept work assignments
payload.justification: Signal SG915 implies claiming as a strategic function necessary for children to engage with the task system.

payload.description: Completion tracking function recording task fulfillment status
payload.justification: Signal SG915 explicitly identifies tracking completion as a strategic function central to the enterprise purpose.

payload.description: Task-reward definition function establishing monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG916 describes defining task-reward relationships as a strategic function parents must perform.

payload.description: Task as completion-trackable work unit with monetary reward
payload.justification: Signals SG915 and SG916 establish tasks as strategic entities central to the enterprise, representing work items that drive the pocket money system.

payload.description: Task-reward relationship binding tasks to monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG916 describes the strategic relationship between tasks and monetary rewards as a definable connection requiring parental control.

payload.description: Achievement as child's tracked completion outcome
payload.justification: Signals SG917 and SG918 identify achievements as strategic entities representing completion outcomes that require visibility and recognition.

payload.description: Periodic financial reporting cycle for transparency delivery
payload.justification: Signal SG918 references periodic transparency, implying a strategic cycle governing when financial information is presented.

payload.description: Child as primary user claiming and tracking task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG915 explicitly identifies children as actors who claim and track tasks, establishing their strategic role in the enterprise.

payload.description: Parent as task-reward definer, monetary value assignor, and oversight/accountability reporting recipient
payload.justification: Signal SG916 clearly describes parents as the strategic actor responsible for defining task-reward relationships and setting monetary values. | Signal SG919 establishes parents as the actor requiring oversight reports and fulfilling payment obligations, distinct from their task-defining role.

payload.justification: Signal SG915 directly expresses this as the core strategic purpose of the enterprise system.

payload.description: Provide children visibility into their task achievements
payload.justification: Signal SG917 identifies achievement visibility as a distinct strategic motivational goal separate from opportunity visibility.

payload.description: Provide children periodic financial transparency
payload.justification: Signal SG918 establishes periodic financial transparency as a strategic goal distinct from real-time task tracking.

payload.description: Provide children achievement recognition
payload.justification: Signal SG918 identifies achievement recognition as a separate strategic goal supporting child motivation and engagement.

payload.justification: Signal SG919 articulates parental oversight as a strategic goal ensuring accountability in the enterprise system.

payload.description: Support parental fulfillment of payment obligations

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Work-Based Earning Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining compensable work units, establishing monetary values for completed efforts, and ensuring transparent linkage between child labor contributions and financial rewards within the household economy.

payload.name: Achievement Recognition Governance
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to capturing, preserving, and acknowledging completion outcomes as formal records of individual contribution, providing visibility into accomplishments and supporting developmental recognition of effort and capability.

payload.name: Child Economic Participation Rights
payload.description: Enterprise recognition of children as economic participants with entitlements to claim work opportunities, track their own completion status, and receive transparent accounting of their earned entitlements within the household financial structure.

payload.name: Parental Oversight Obligation
payload.description: Enterprise obligation for authoritative supervision of work-reward structures, accountability reporting on financial commitments, and fulfillment of payment obligations arising from completed work within the governed household economic system.

payload.name: Financial Transparency Commitment
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to periodic disclosure of financial status, earned entitlements, and obligation fulfillment, ensuring participants receive timely and comprehensible accounting of their economic standing and transaction history.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing work obligations, their monetary worth, and ensuring task availability recurs appropriately within operational cycles.

payload.name: Earnings Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for recognizing completed work, attributing it to the performer, and determining resulting financial obligations within the applicable timeframe.

payload.name: Payment Obligation Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling oversight of financial obligations owed, providing visibility into amounts due based on completed work.

payload.name: Performance Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving evidence of work performed and obligations fulfilled beyond immediate operational periods for reference and governance purposes.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise tasks as work units with monetary rewards.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural definition of task as a trackable work unit with associated monetary value at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish the relationship binding tasks to monetary values.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural association between tasks and their corresponding monetary rewards.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish monetary values for tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to define and assign monetary values to work units.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall be accountable to parents for task-reward definition and monetary value assignment.
payload.rationale: Establishes enterprise governance and accountability constraint identifying parents as the responsible actors for task-reward stewardship and oversight reporting.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to provide the capability for children to claim work units.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to track task completion.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to provide visibility into completion status of claimed work units.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into available tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses enterprise commitment to make task opportunities visible to children.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a record of each child's task completion outcomes.
payload.rationale: Derived from the entity definition of achievement as a tracked completion outcome and the completion tracking function. The enterprise commits to recording and preserving task fulfillment status as a formal record.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into their task completion outcomes.
payload.rationale: Derived from the goal to provide children visibility into their task achievements. The enterprise commits to enabling access to completion records.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognize children's task completion outcomes.
payload.rationale: Derived from the goal to provide achievement recognition. The enterprise commits to acknowledging completion outcomes as a form of developmental recognition.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Core enterprise commitment to provide children the capability to accept work assignments. Derived from the task claiming function (How), the child actor role (Who), and the goal to enable task claiming (Why).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to track task completion.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to provide children visibility into their own completion status. Derived from the child actor's tracking role and the goal to enable completion tracking.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable children to view their achievements and completion history.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children periodic financial transparency.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to periodic disclosure of earnings and financial status to child participants within the household economic structure.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parents as authoritative definers of task-reward structures and assignors of monetary value within the household.
payload.rationale: Defines the governance role and authority boundary for parents as the enterprise accountability point for work-reward definitions. This is a Who-column actor constraint establishing the authoritative role, not a function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to receive oversight and accountability reports on household financial commitments.
payload.rationale: Derived from the parent's role as oversight and accountability reporting recipient (Who-002) and the goal of enabling parental oversight (Why-006). This is a capability the enterprise must provide.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide periodic financial reporting for transparency to parents.
payload.rationale: When-column cycle CCI combined with the transparency and accountability goal. The enterprise commits to a recurring capability to deliver financial reports.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to fulfill payment obligations arising from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing a capability the enterprise must support: enabling payment obligation fulfillment. Though Why-column, the content is capability-oriented rather than a governance constraint.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children periodic financial transparency.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal establishing enterprise commitment to periodic transparency delivery; When-column cycle specifies the periodic nature of the obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parental oversight and accountability reporting.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal establishing enterprise commitment to governance transparency for parental stakeholders.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Describes the core purpose of enabling children to claim and track completion-based pocket money tasks.
Identifies the parental motivation to define task-reward relationships with monetary values.
Reflects the motivational goal of providing children visibility into available opportunities and their achievements.
Expresses the purpose of providing children periodic financial transparency and achievement recognition.
Articulates the parental need for oversight and accountability reporting to fulfill payment obligations.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system overview content.
Core business concept defining the system as a mechanism enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks on a weekly cycle.
Business capability identifying parents as actors who define tasks and their associated monetary values.
Conceptual relationship requirement establishing that completed tasks must be associated with the child who performed them.
Business capability specifying children as actors with visibility into both available and their own completed tasks within the current week.
Business rule defining weekly earning totals as a calculated conceptual entity visible to children at week end.
Business capability enabling parents to access aggregated reports of child earnings and completed tasks for payment fulfillment.
Business rule establishing the weekly reset cycle where all tasks return to available status at week commencement.
Data retention requirement specifying that historical task information must persist as a conceptual entity beyond current week.
